No Poverty.reason: No course content or learning outcomes directly or indirectly relate to poverty eradication or economic resources for the poor.

Zero Hunger.reason: The syllabus contains no information pertaining to hunger, food security, nutrition, or sustainable agriculture.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: There is no evidence in the course description or objectives that relates to health, well-being, disease prevention, or healthcare services.

Quality Education.reason: The course is university-level (4.3), develops professional communication skills for employment (4.4), and explicitly aims for students to 'understand and be able to identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues' (4.7).
evidence: Business Writing & Oral Training I provides speaking and writing training at the university level
understand and be able to identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues

Gender Equality.reason: The syllabus does not address gender-specific issues, discrimination, or the empowerment of women and girls.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No content in the syllabus relates to water quality, sanitation, hygiene, or water resource management.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: The course does not cover topics related to energy access, renewable energy, or energy efficiency.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course's focus on 'Business Writing & Oral Training' and 'professional communication skills' directly contributes to developing skills for decent job creation and productive employment (Targets 8.3, 8.5).
evidence: Business Writing and Oral Training I
C. 溝通能力(Students will acquire professional communication skills.)

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: There is no mention of infrastructure development, industrialization, or fostering innovation in the course materials.

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course does not explicitly address reducing inequalities based on income, age, sex, disability, race, or other status.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No course content relates to urban planning, housing, transport, or the protection of cultural and natural heritage.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: Core competence B, 'understand and be able to identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues,' aligns with fostering awareness for sustainable development (Target 12.8).
evidence: B. 理解與辨別道德、社會責任與永續議題(Students will understand and be able to identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues.)

Climate Action.reason: While 'sustainability issues' are mentioned, there is no specific focus on climate change mitigation, adaptation, or awareness-raising related to climate action.

Life Below Water.reason: The syllabus does not include any topics related to marine ecosystems, ocean health, or sustainable use of marine resources.

Life on Land.reason: No course content addresses terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, deforestation, or land degradation.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course teaches 'Assertive Communication' and 'Active Listening,' which are foundational skills for effective, participatory decision-making and strong institutions (Targets 16.6, 16.7).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course explicitly aims to 'develop team spirit' and 'work effectively in teams,' which are foundational skills for fostering partnerships (Target 17.17), even if at a classroom level.
evidence: work individually... and also in groups to develop team spirit
E. 團隊合作與領導能力(Students will be able to work effectively in teams...)
